name: MeshCore
label: MeshCore (mainline) — companion LoRa mesh
description: meshcore-dev/MeshCore — hybrid flood+routed LoRa mesh (companion role) for ESP32 / S3 / C3 / C6 boards. Lawful off-grid comms; no attack, jammer, or deauth. Cyber Controller flashes the mainline merged image and adds no capability of its own.
note: MAINLINE MeshCore (meshcore-dev, MIT) — distinct from CC's 'MCLite (MeshCore fork)' which is the smaller laserir/MCLite fork. Pinned to the companion role tag companion-v1.16.0: MeshCore publishes each role (companion / repeater / room-server) as its OWN release tag, so multi-role and ble-vs-usb selection is a deferred resolver enhancement (github_release resolves ONE api_url today); this profile flashes the companion merged image and prefers the BLE variant. Uses the -merged.bin (flash @0x0), never an app-only .bin. asset_match is a REGEX pinned to EXACTLY the 5 declared boards' companion_radio_ble merged images (Heltec_v3 / Station_G2 / Xiao_C3 / M5Stack_Unit_C6L / Tbeam_SX1262) — this closes the wrong-chip-default hole where the old '-merged.bin' suffix match pulled in all 61 assets and let the esp32 default land on an S3 image (Ebyte EoRa-S3) for a declared classic board. With the narrowed set each board resolves to its correct chip: heltec_v3->s3, xiao_c3->c3, m5stack_unit_c6l->c6 by rule; Station_G2 + Tbeam_SX1262 -> esp32 default (both genuinely classic, no S3/C3/C6 stragglers left in the esp32 bucket). Offset 0x0 kept verify: until a real Heltec V3 / Station G2 flash confirms it (Phase F hardware gate).
